evals.prompt: Using the attached scientific use-cases file, prepare a targeted literature-discovery plan and candidate shortlist format for Use Case 1, the IL-2 pathway modulation use case. Do not extract quantitative endpoints or propose calibrated parameter values yet.
evals.expected_output: Clarifies the search frame, builds an Entity Table with canonical names / synonyms / mesh terms / related entities / intent groups / exclusions, decides which of the three intent groups (Knowledge / Data / Models) the request spans, proposes 3-5 angled PubMed queries per entity per group using PubMed primitives ([mh], [pt], [ta], [PDat], NOT clauses), plans the rounds + saturation loop with an author / venue seeding step, applies verification heuristics for Data / Models candidates, and returns a structured shortlist schema labeled by intent group.
expectations: The output builds an Entity Table with canonical names, synonyms, MeSH terms, related entities, intent groups, and exclusions (e.g. IL-2 / interleukin-2 / IL2, regulatory T cells / Tregs, SLE / systemic lupus erythematosus).
The output names the intent group(s) (Knowledge, Data, Models) the search should cover.
The output proposes 3-5 angled queries per entity per intent group (triangulation), not a single omnibus query.
The output uses PubMed primitives such as [mh], [mh:noexp], [pt], [ta], [PDat], [lang], humans[mh], and NOT clauses rather than bare keyword queries.

Knowledge queries prioritize Review[pt] and high-impact venues via [ta]; Data queries split by evidence type and add publication-type filters; Model queries explicitly include NOT (animals[mh] NOT humans[mh]) and a NOT clause against risk scores / prognostic models.
The output plans rounds (Orientation, Refinement with author/venue seeding, Gap-filling) and states a saturation stopping criterion.

The output describes a verification step with concrete heuristics (Table 1 / Figure 1 / units / N= / endpoints for Data; equations / parameters / Supporting Information / BioModels / SBML for Models).
The output returns a structured shortlist schema with intent_group, evidence_type, entities, verification_passed, verification_note, priority, priority_rationale, and query_provenance per candidate.
The output states that quantitative extraction and calibration planning are downstream handoffs, not completed by this skill.

evals.prompt: I want a literature search to build understanding of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD), so I can later draft a knowledge investigation. Please plan the search.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the intent as Knowledge, builds an Entity Table for ASCVD with related entities (cholesterol, plaque, LDL), constructs 3-5 angled queries using PubMed primitives (Review[pt], [mh:major], top-journal [ta] filters), plans rounds with author / venue seeding, and proposes a connected-paper expansion after Round 1.
expectations: The output identifies the intent as Knowledge and not Data or Models.

The output builds an Entity Table for ASCVD with multiple synonyms (ASCVD, atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, atherosclerosis, coronary artery disease) and related entities (cholesterol, plaque, LDL).

The output proposes 3-5 angled queries combining MeSH ([mh] / [mh:major]), publication-type (Review[pt] / Practice Guideline[pt] / Meta-Analysis[pt]), venue ([ta] for Nature Reviews / Lancet / NEJM / Annual Reviews), and date-range filters.
The output plans paired-keyword passes from the related-entities column (role of cholesterol in plaque, impact of LDL on plaque).
The output plans Round 1 -> Author/venue seeding -> Round 2 -> Round 3 with explicit saturation triggers (% new candidates, target count, no new authors/journals).
The output proposes using jk-connected-paper after the first useful batch (placeholder skill is acceptable).
The output does not narrow the search to PK / PBPK / pharmacometrics by default.

evals.prompt: I need data to inform a population PK/PD model of semaglutide exposure-response in obesity. Please give me a focused discovery workflow and the CLI command you would run if network access is available.
evals.expected_output: Frames the request as a Data search for clinical-trial and clinical-study evidence, builds an Entity Table for semaglutide (with Ozempic / Wegovy / Rybelsus) and obesity, builds angled queries that use Clinical Trial Phase III[pt] / Phase II[pt] / Observational Study[pt] and humans[mh], applies verification heuristics for in-trial outcome tables (endpoints, week-X values, dose arms, change from baseline, PK metrics Cmax / AUC / Tmax / half-life), and provides a valid literature_search.py command.
expectations: The output identifies the intent as Data and confirms the evidence type (clinical trial Phase II / Phase III and / or clinical study).
The output builds an Entity Table including semaglutide / Ozempic / Wegovy / Rybelsus joined with OR, plus obesity / overweight / BMI synonyms.
The output writes angled queries using Clinical Trial Phase III[pt] / Phase II[pt] / Phase I[pt] / Observational Study[pt] and humans[mh].

The output describes verification heuristics that explicitly include clinical-trial-data signals: endpoint terms, week-X timepoints, dose arms, change-from-baseline, AE tables, PK metrics (Cmax / AUC / Tmax / half-life).
The output includes a valid literature_search.py CLI command referencing skills/jk-task-literature-search/.
The output proposes a jk-connected-paper expansion after Round 1.
The output avoids claiming that identified papers contain extracted numeric data unless inspected.

evals.prompt: I want to find reference mathematical models of type 2 diabetes glucose homeostasis I could re-implement. Plan the literature search.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the intent as Models, asks or assumes the model granularity (e.g. QSP, mechanistic, ODE-based, Pop-PK / PD), builds an Entity Table for type 2 diabetes and glucose homeostasis, builds angled Model queries with explicit NOT clauses against animal models and statistical risk scores, and applies verification heuristics for reusable model artifacts.
expectations: The output identifies the intent as Models.
The output asks or states an assumption about model granularity (PK / PBPK / Pop-PK / QSP / mechanistic).
The output builds Model angled queries using patterns such as mathematical model[tiab], computational model[tiab], mechanistic model[tiab], QSP[tiab], PBPK[tiab], ODE[tiab].
Each Model query explicitly contains NOT (animals[mh] NOT humans[mh]) and NOT (risk score[tiab] OR prognostic model[tiab] OR prognostic score[tiab]).
The output describes verification heuristics specific to Models: equation / ODE / compartment / parameter table / Supporting Information / BioModels / SBML / GitHub / Zenodo / validated against.
The output prioritizes papers that report equations, parameter values, or supplementary code for reimplementation.

evals.prompt: I want a thorough Knowledge + Data search on chronic hepatitis B for a future combination-therapy QSP model. Plan the rounds and tell me when you would stop.
evals.expected_output: Frames a multi-intent search (Knowledge + Data) on chronic hepatitis B with a single shared Entity Table; lays out Round 1 (Orientation per group), Author / Venue seeding, Round 2 (Refinement using Round 1 learnings + seeded author / journal queries), Round 3 (Gap-filling for under-represented evidence types or granularities); states the saturation triggers (less than ~20% new after dedup over two rounds, per-group target counts, no new authors / journals); and shows cross-intent consistency.
expectations: The output uses a single shared Entity Table across Knowledge and Data batches (cross-intent consistency).
The output runs Knowledge and Data angled-query batches in parallel within each round, not sequentially.
The output explicitly names the three rounds: Orientation, Refinement, Gap-filling.
The output performs author / venue seeding between Round 1 and Round 2, using the top authors / journals from Round 1 result sets.
The output states the saturation stopping criteria: less than ~20% new candidates after dedup over two consecutive rounds, per-group target counts (default 10 / 10 / 5), no new authors / journals.
The output records the saturation decision in manifest.json (or equivalent).
The output keeps the disease / treatment / population canonical names consistent across the Knowledge and Data shortlists.

evals.prompt: I already have five papers and want you to digitize their plots, create calibration tables, and tell me the first parameters to fit for a PBPK model. Is jk-task-literature-search the right skill?
evals.expected_output: Explains that jk-task-literature-search is only for discovery and shortlist preparation, not OCR, digitization, quantitative extraction, or calibration execution, and suggests appropriate handoffs (jk-mistral-ocr, jk-task-extract-data-table, jk-data-table, jk-model) while still offering to organize the provided papers as a candidate inventory using the structured shortlist schema.
expectations: The output says quantitative extraction, plot digitization, and calibration execution are out of scope.
The output offers a useful in-scope alternative such as organizing or prioritizing the provided papers using the structured shortlist schema and labeling them by intent group.
The output mentions relevant downstream handoffs such as jk-mistral-ocr, jk-task-extract-data-table, jk-data-table, or jk-model.
The output does not reference misspelled or nonexistent skill names.
